title: Engineering Mathematics - Linear Algebra - Eigen Value

questions.question:
Which of the following statements about the eigen values of \( I_n \), the \( n \times n \) identity matrix (over complex numbers), is true?



Linear Algebra: TIFR CSE 2016 | Part A | Question: 6
options: The eigen values are \( 1, \omega, \omega^2, \dots , \omega^{n-1} \), where \( \omega \) is a primitive \( n \)-th root of unity
Both \( 0 \) and \( 1 \) are eigen values, but there are no other eigen values

questions.question:
\( A \) is \( n \times n \) square matrix for which the entries in every row sum to \( 1 \). Consider the following statements:

  1. The column vector \( [1,1,\ldots,1]^T \) is an eigen vector of \( A. \)
  2. \( \text{det}(A-I) = 0. \)
  3. \( \text{det}(A) = 0. \)

Which of the above statements must be TRUE?



Linear Algebra: TIFR CSE 2019 | Part A | Question: 3

Consider the following matrix:
\[
A=\left[\begin{array}{lll}
3 & 0 & 1 \\
0 & 2 & 0 \\
x & 0 & 1
\end{array}\right]
\]
If all eigenvalues of the matrix are the same, what is the value of \( x \) ?



Linear Algebra: TIFR CSE 2025 | Part A | Question: 6

Let \( m, n \) be positive integers such that \( 10 \leq m \leq n \). Let \( A \) be an \( m \times n \) matrix with real entries. Let \( v \) be a unit vector in \( \mathbb{R}^{n} \) with the maximum possible value of \( \|A v\|_{2} \). Define \( \ell:=\|A v\|_{2}^{2} \) and let \( \lambda \) be an eigenvalue of \( A^{T} A \) with the largest absolute value. Which of the following is \( \text{TRUE}? \) (Here, for a vect or \( u,\|u\|_{2} \) represents its Euclidean length.)



Linear Algebra: TIFR CSE 2025 | Part B | Question: 12
options: \( v \) always exists, but \( \lambda \) may not be real.
\( v \) always exists, \( \lambda \) is real, and \( \lambda=\ell \)
\( v \) always exists, \( \lambda \) is real, \( \lambda \leq \ell \), and \( \lambda \neq \ell \) is possible.
\( v \) always exists, \( \lambda \) is real, \( \lambda \geq \ell \), and \( \lambda \neq \ell \) is possible.
questions.correct_answer: \( v \) always exists, \( \lambda \) is real, and \( \lambda=\ell \)
